title: The Forty-Five Minute Gap in Farrow's Account
description: When Lieutenant Farrow's stated timeline is compared against Beaumont's telephone log, Voss's sighting, and the wire-cut timeline, a gap of 45 minutes emerges — from 04:00 to 04:45 — during which Farrow is unaccounted for by any witness. The distance from the artillery dugout to splice point Delta-7 and back is exactly 38 minutes at a fast walk through the communication trenches. The gap fits.
